growth_records.strain: mixed community (Hainich CZE well H41 groundwater)
growth_records.medium: 13C-bicarbonate + thiosulfate amended Hainich CZE groundwater microcosm (well H41)
growth_records.cite: Taubert, Martin et al. Bolstering fitness via CO2 fixation and organic carbon uptake: mixotrophs in modern groundwater. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01163-x. [PMC8941145]
growth_records.snippet: During the first three weeks of incubation, thiosulfate and oxygen consumption rates remained relatively low (1.7 +/- 1.9 and 5.5 +/- 2.0 umol d-1 (mean +/- SD), respectively... After 70 days of incubation, consumption rates of thiosulfate
growth_records.notes: Community-level (not per-organism, not per-gDW) measured consumption rates. Thiosulfate: 1.7 +/- 1.9 umol/d (first 3 weeks) rising to 7.2 +/- 2.0 umol/d (day 70). Oxygen: 5.5 +/- 2.0 rising to 12.8 +/- 3.2 umol/d. O2:thiosulfate ratio ~2:1, near-theoretical 2:1:2 (O2:S2O3:SO4) stoichiometry for oxygen-dependent thiosulfate oxidation. Cannot compute biomass-specific qS (no biomass/gDW reported); rates retained verbatim in umol/d per microcosm.
